feature.name: QuickBooks Migration Requirements
feature.description: Document QuickBooks integration migration from FileMaker to backend/Supabase

standing_constraints: DO NOT modify backend infrastructure or database schema
Follow Backend Change Protocol for any backend requirements
Document only - do not implement code changes yet
Reference existing code files with line numbers

tasks.title: Create README.md with QuickBooks overview
tasks.description: Create requirements/quickbooks/README.md documenting current QuickBooks integration, FileMaker dependencies, and migration objectives
tasks.implementation_notes: Implemented a comprehensive QuickBooks integration requirements document in 'requirements/quickbooks/README.md' (about 1,044 lines) capturing the current architecture (including 'quickbooksApi.js' HMAC-SHA256 auth and invoice business rules), FileMaker dependencies (via 'financialSyncService.js' and the 'devRecords' layout), and the full data flow through Supabase to QuickBooks. The README also specifies migration objectives to remove FileMaker reliance, including a new 'useCustomerSales' hook, files to deprecate, and a detailed acceptance criteria and test plan, while explicitly deciding on no backend code changes per the backend change protocol. Verified the file creation and confirmed the project builds successfully with no errors.

tasks.title: Document current QuickBooks implementation
tasks.description: Create current-implementation.md analyzing initializeQuickBooks function, FileMaker script behavior, existing quickbooksApi.js client, and UI integration points
tasks.implementation_notes: Created a comprehensive 'current-implementation.md' (1,028 lines) documenting the existing QuickBooks integration in Clarity CRM Frontend, including architecture (FileMaker WebViewer vs standalone), data flows, auth, limitations, and the migration direction. The work referenced and summarized key existing implementations in 'src/api/fileMaker.js' (including 'initializeQuickBooks'), 'src/api/quickbooksApi.js' (backend-integrated client with HMAC-SHA256 and OAuth flow), and 'src/services/invoiceGenerationService.js' (invoice numbering, tax rules, due dates, and line grouping), plus UI touchpoints like 'src/components/financial/CustomerSalesTable.jsx' and sync services. No code changes were made; the build was verified to compile successfully, and the main decision was to produce a documentation-only, line-number-accurate snapshot to support subsequent migration planning.

tasks.title: Map QuickBooks data model requirements
tasks.description: Create data-model-mapping.md documenting token storage requirements, organization configuration, QB entity mappings, and Supabase schema needs
tasks.implementation_notes: Implemented a comprehensive QuickBooks data model mapping specification to support migrating the integration from FileMaker to a Supabase-only architecture, covering token storage, org-level QB configuration, entity mappings (customers, invoices, items), transformation rules, and backend change requirements. The primary deliverable is the new or updated document at 'requirements/quickbooks/data-model-mapping.md' (about 986 lines), which includes proposed DDL for new tables like 'quickbooks_tokens', 'organization_quickbooks_config', and 'quickbooks_sync_log', plus required schema updates to existing tables such as 'customers' and 'customer_sales' with RLS and indexing guidance. Key decisions included moving hardcoded QuickBooks settings (tax codes, item IDs, invoice defaults) into database-driven configuration and defining a deprecation strategy for the FileMaker 'financial_id'. The session also verified referenced implementation points against existing code in 'src/api/quickbooksApi.js', 'src/services/invoiceGenerationService.js', and 'src/services/financialSyncService.js', and confirmed task status in 'tasks.json' was already marked 'done'.

tasks.title: Define QuickBooks API contracts
tasks.description: Create api-contracts.md specifying backend endpoints for QB initialization, OAuth flow, token refresh, invoice generation, customer sync, and testing operations
tasks.implementation_notes: Implemented a comprehensive QuickBooks API contracts specification document at '.devflow/tasks/quickbooks-migration-requirements/api-contracts.md', documenting 30 endpoints across OAuth, customers, invoices, reference data, bills, and utilities with TypeScript request and response schemas, auth requirements, business rules, and error handling. Updated the document to replace initially estimated source references with verified, accurate line number and file references by cross-checking actual function definitions and webhook handlers in the codebase. Verified correctness by re-checking key business rules and item mappings to avoid hallucinated values, then confirmed the project builds successfully via 'npm run build'. Updated task status in 'tasks.json' to mark the documentation work complete with implementation notes.

tasks.title: Document authorization requirements
tasks.description: Create authorization.md covering OAuth flow, token lifecycle, organization scoping, RLS policies, and security considerations
tasks.implementation_notes: Implemented comprehensive QuickBooks authorization documentation by adding 'requirements/quickbooks/authorization.md', covering the 3-layer auth model (QuickBooks OAuth 2.0, backend HMAC-SHA256, and Supabase JWT with RLS), token lifecycle, org scoping, RLS policy expectations, error handling, and security considerations. Verified all referenced behaviors and line numbers against existing code in 'src/api/quickbooksApi.js', 'src/services/supabaseService.js', and 'src/services/dataService.js', and updated the doc to reflect the decision that 'disconnect' is backend-only and not exposed via the frontend client. No runtime code changes were made; the project build was run to confirm documentation-only updates did not break compilation.

tasks.title: Create migration plan
tasks.description: Create migration-plan.md with step-by-step approach to migrate from FileMaker scripts to backend API, including rollback procedures and cutover strategy
tasks.implementation_notes: Created comprehensive migration plan document 'migration-plan.md' (650+ lines) with a 4-phase approach to migrate QuickBooks invoice generation from FileMaker scripts to backend API. The plan includes: (1) Preparation phase with code audit, backend API verification, data model validation, and test data creation; (2) Backend implementation phase (determined no changes needed - all APIs exist); (3) Frontend migration phase with detailed service layer refactoring (new qbInvoiceService.js), component updates, timer integration changes, and comprehensive testing strategy; (4) Cutover and validation phase with deployment steps, monitoring metrics, and validation tests. Included rollback procedures for immediate, partial, and emergency scenarios; performance benchmarks comparing FileMaker (fire-and-forget) vs backend API (5s target); risk mitigation strategies for 6 identified risks; and success metrics for technical, UX, and business outcomes. The plan strictly adheres to Backend Change Protocol (no backend modifications), includes no time estimates per CLAUDE.md guidelines, follows Teams Migration pattern from BACKEND_CHANGE_REQUEST_002, references existing code with line numbers, and includes code examples throughout. Verified project builds successfully with no errors.

tasks.title: Define acceptance criteria and test plan
tasks.description: Create acceptance-criteria.md with test cases covering OAuth flow, token refresh, invoice generation, error handling, retry logic, and edge cases

tasks.title: Create backend change request document
tasks.description: Create BACKEND_CHANGE_REQUEST_001_QUICKBOOKS_MIGRATION.md following the backend change protocol with SQL, API contracts, testing requirements, and rollback plan
